hot_topics.topic: Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger Halted by Judge
hot_topics.synthesis: A federal judge has issued a temporary restraining order to block the proposed $111 billion merger between Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros. Discovery. This action follows a lawsuit from 12 states, including California, which argue that the deal would significantly reduce competition within the Hollywood movie studio and basic cable television industries. The ruling represents a significant early victory for those challenging the megadeal, aiming to prevent potential monopolistic effects.

hot_topics.topic: The Resurgence of Minimalist Flip Phones
hot_topics.synthesis: The Light company has introduced a new minimalist flip phone, the Light Flip, aiming to provide an alternative to smartphones for users seeking fewer distractions and a device that doesn't resemble a smartphone. This colorful and affordable new model reflects a surprising return to relevance for flip phones, with Light's co-founder noting the renewed appeal of the form factor.

hot_topics.topic: Xteink's Enhanced Portable E-Reader with Touchscreen and Light
hot_topics.synthesis: Xteink has launched its X4 Pro e-reader, featuring a touchscreen and front light, making it the most user-friendly and portable device in the company's lineup. Despite some hardware compromises, these additions, along with anticipated support for excellent third-party firmware, significantly improve the reading experience for the pocket-sized device. These enhancements make the X4 Pro a compelling choice for those prioritizing portability in an e-reader.

hot_topics.topic: Nativ Enables Local AI Model Execution on Mac
hot_topics.synthesis: A new macOS desktop application called Nativ allows users to run advanced open-weight AI models locally on their Apple Silicon Macs, providing a chat interface and a localhost API server. Developed by Prince Canuma, this tool leverages existing MLX libraries to make large language models more accessible for local execution. It offers a solution similar to LM Studio, bringing frontier AI capabilities to Mac users' desktops.

hot_topics.topic: Escalating US-Iran Tensions and Houthi Maritime Blockade
hot_topics.synthesis: Tensions between the US and Iran are sharply escalating, with the US launching new strikes following President Trump's warnings of retaliation for soldier deaths. In a related development, Yemen's Iran-backed Houthi rebels have announced an immediate maritime embargo on Saudi Arabia and threatened to blockade the crucial Bab el-Mandeb Strait. These actions further heighten regional instability and maritime security concerns in a critical global waterway.

hot_topics.topic: Widespread Protests in India for Social and Education Reform
hot_topics.synthesis: India is experiencing ongoing, youth-led "Cockroach movement" protests, which began as satirical but have grown into a significant challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government, despite police crackdowns injuring dozens. Separately, thousands of mostly young people are demonstrating to demand comprehensive reforms to the country's education system. These widespread protests highlight deep public dissatisfaction with current political and social policies.

hot_topics.topic: New US Tariffs on Canadian Goods Intensify Trade Tensions
hot_topics.synthesis: President Donald Trump has imposed steep 50% tariffs on a range of Canadian goods, including autos, alcohol, and dairy products, claiming unfair discrimination against American products. This move marks a significant escalation in trade tensions between the United States and Canada. Canadian officials have pledged to intensify trade negotiations in response to these duties, seeking to address the friction.

hot_topics.topic: Global Debate on Open-Weight AI Models and China's Strategy
hot_topics.synthesis: The AI industry is grappling with the implications of open-weight AI models, with discussions around potentially banning Chinese-made LLMs revealing the challenges of commercializing AI and geopolitical competition. China's strategy of developing and sharing open-weight AI models is reportedly gaining significant traction and success. This approach contrasts sharply with the proprietary and locked-down models often developed by American AI companies like OpenAI, raising questions about US policy in this evolving landscape.

items.title: The people’s atheist
items.summary: Jean Meslier, an 18th-century French priest, secretly preached atheism, arguing that religion was a tool designed to ensure obedience among the populace. His writings reflect a deep critique of religious authority and societal control.

items.title: 2027 Kia Seltos first drive gives you plenty of SUV for not much $
items.summary: The 2027 Kia Seltos, a new subcompact SUV, offers a Telluride-inspired design at an affordable starting price of $24,990. While a 1.6L hybrid variant is planned, details and a first drive for it are pending EPA certification.

items.title: AliExpress hit with record $625M fine after failing to make EU-ordered fixes
items.summary: AliExpress was fined over $625 million by the European Commission under the Digital Services Act for failing to mitigate risks related to the sale of illegal, unsafe, or counterfeit products. The platform reportedly lacked sufficient teams to remove dangerous items and allowed bad actors to evade detection.

items.title: AT&T loses key ruling in bid to stop offering basic phone service in California
items.summary: A federal judge ruled that California can continue enforcing "Carrier of Last Resort" rules, requiring AT&T to offer basic phone service to new customers in its wireline territory. AT&T's request for a preliminary injunction against these rules was denied.

items.title: F1 in Belgium: Machine learning algorithms are ruining the sport
items.summary: Formula 1's 2026 cars are reportedly ill-suited for historic tracks like Spa-Francorchamps, with their battery systems limiting electric motor power for much of the lap. This issue makes the cars less powerful than junior series vehicles on tracks demanding sustained energy.

items.title: Judge halts Paramount's $111B purchase of Warner Bros. in win for US states
items.summary: A federal judge temporarily halted Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros. Discovery's $111 billion merger, granting a restraining order in response to a lawsuit from 12 states. The states argue the deal would eliminate competition between major Hollywood studios and cable TV channel owners.

items.title: RIP bargain bin: The price impact of Sony's disc-free PlayStation plan
items.summary: Sony's plan to stop producing physical PlayStation discs by 2028 raises concerns about game ownership and the loss of cheap used disc markets. An analysis showed that while used discs are often cheaper, PlayStation Store's frequent deep discounts frequently undercut them, suggesting digital pricing may remain competitive.

items.title: When your vehicle outlives its cloud: What happens next?
items.summary: Cloud-based "connected services" in vehicles, offering features like remote control and emergency calls, are becoming obsolete as cars age, leaving drivers without previously enjoyed functionalities. These modern data-driven systems, evolving from 1990s voice-centric options, depend on centralized back-end support that has a limited lifespan.

items.title: 'Anti-ICE' army veteran started fire outside Manhattan federal building, say officials
items.summary: An "Anti-ICE" army veteran, Andrew Arrabaca, allegedly started a fire outside a Manhattan federal building while armed with multiple weapons, according to FBI officials. This incident suggests political motivation and raises security concerns.

items.title: At least 27 dead and 83 missing after passenger ferry capsizes off Guyana
items.summary: A passenger ferry capsized off Guyana, resulting in at least 27 deaths and 83 missing, prompting an investigation into the tragedy by the island's prime minister. Search and rescue efforts are underway.

items.title: Dozens hurt in Bologna protests after man dies while being restrained by police
items.summary: Dozens were injured in protests in Bologna after a Moroccan-born man died while being restrained by police, prompting thousands to demand justice. The incident has led to widespread civil unrest.

items.title: India's 'cockroach' protest continues a day after police crackdown
items.summary: India's "cockroach" protest movement is continuing despite a police crackdown that resulted in dozens of injuries to both protesters and police. The movement, initially satirical, is challenging Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.

items.title: Israeli air strike in Gaza City kills family of six, medics say
items.summary: An Israeli air strike in Gaza City reportedly killed a family of six, including Firas al-Masri, his wife, and their four children, though the Israeli military stated it targeted a Hamas operative. This incident highlights civilian casualties in ongoing conflicts.

items.title: Lebanese army says troops deploying in 'pilot zone' after Israeli withdrawal
items.summary: The Lebanese army is deploying troops in a "pilot zone" after an Israeli withdrawal, facilitated by a US-brokered agreement. This move follows an Israeli conflict with Hezbollah in a small part of Lebanon.

items.title: Notorious drug cartel leader Ismael Zambada sentenced to life in US prison
items.summary: Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada, co-founder of the Sinaloa Cartel, was sentenced to life in a US prison and ordered to pay $15 billion after admitting to trafficking vast amounts of drugs to the US. This marks a major blow against organized crime.

items.title: Police officers arrested for allegedly seeking roadside bribe from Nigeria's anti-corruption boss
items.summary: Nigerian police officers have been arrested for allegedly attempting to solicit a bribe from Musa Aliyu, the country's anti-corruption boss, at a fake roadblock. This incident highlights ongoing corruption issues within the police force.

items.title: Row escalates in Ukraine over army chief's future
items.summary: A political row is escalating in Ukraine concerning the future of army chief Oleksandr Syrskyi, with protesters demanding his removal and the reinstatement of former defense minister Mykhailo Fedorov. This indicates internal dissent regarding military leadership.

items.title: Shock and anger after top Ghanaian politician sentenced to 20 years for illegal mining
items.summary: Bernard Antwi Boasiako, a prominent Ghanaian politician known as "Chairman Wontumi," was sentenced to 20 years for illegal mining and fined over $10,000, causing widespread shock and anger. The sentencing addresses corruption and environmental issues.

items.title: Tate brothers appear in US court for extradition proceedings
items.summary: The Tate brothers appeared in a US court for extradition proceedings after being arrested for dozens of new charges in the UK, including rape and sex trafficking, which they deny. The case involves international legal action.

items.title: Trump slaps 50% tariffs on Canada and Carney vows to 'intensify' trade talks
items.summary: President Trump imposed 50% tariffs on Canadian goods, escalating trade tensions between the two North American nations. Canada's Carney has pledged to intensify trade talks in response to these duties.

items.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
items.summary: The US launched new strikes on Iran following Trump's warning of retaliation for soldier deaths, while Iran claimed it hit ships in the Strait of Hormuz and targets in Bahrain and Jordan. This marks a significant escalation in regional tensions.

items.title: Watch Texas police officer pull man from burning car
items.summary: Bodycam and dashcam footage captured a Texas police officer rescuing a man from a burning car moments before it became engulfed in flames. The footage highlights an act of heroism.

items.title: Watch: Flooded towns and evacuations as Chile hit by heavy rain
items.summary: Heavy rain in Chile has caused flooding in towns and led to evacuations, prompting authorities to declare a "state of catastrophe" in Coquimbo and Huasco. The event highlights severe weather impacts.

items.title: Why are thousands protesting for education reform in India?
items.summary: Thousands of mostly young people in India are protesting, attempting to march on parliament to demand reforms to the education system. The protests highlight widespread dissatisfaction with current educational policies.

items.title: Yemen's Houthis announce 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia
items.summary: Yemen's Houthis have declared an immediate "maritime embargo" on Saudi Arabia, stating it is a response to Saudi blockades of Yemeni ports and airports. This escalates regional tensions and maritime security concerns.

items.title: Agent swarms and the new model economics
items.summary: The concept of "agent swarms" is emerging as a significant factor in the new economics of AI models. This suggests a shift in how AI systems are deployed and interact, potentially impacting resource allocation and cost.

items.title: China’s open-weights AI strategy is winning
items.summary: China's strategy of developing and sharing open-weights AI models is reportedly gaining traction and success. This approach contrasts with the proprietary, locked-down nature of much American AI.

items.title: Human mathematicians are being outcounterexampled
items.summary: Human mathematicians are increasingly being "outcounterexampled" by automated systems, suggesting that AI or computational tools are becoming more adept at finding counterexamples to mathematical conjectures. This indicates a shift in mathematical discovery methods.

items.title: I Stopped “Creating Content”
items.summary: A personal account describes the decision to stop "creating content," reflecting a potential shift in perspective on digital production or online engagement. The article likely explores reasons and implications of this choice.

items.title: I wrote an bash enumerator because I was sick of xargs
items.summary: A developer created a new bash enumerator as an alternative to `xargs`, indicating a desire for more efficient or user-friendly command-line tools. This aims to improve scripting workflows.

items.title: Incremental – A library for incremental computations
items.summary: Incremental is a new library designed for facilitating incremental computations, allowing for efficient updates when only small parts of a data structure change. This can improve performance in certain software applications.

items.title: Jelly UI: Soft-body physics for native HTML form controls
items.summary: Jelly UI introduces soft-body physics to native HTML form controls, aiming to enhance user interface interactions with more dynamic and visually engaging elements. This project explores advanced web animation.

items.title: Kimi Work
items.summary: Kimi Work is a new product or service, attracting significant attention on Hacker News. Details from the title suggest it is related to work or productivity, potentially leveraging AI.

items.title: Nativ: Run frontier open models locally on your Mac
items.summary: Nativ is a new project that allows users to run "frontier open models" locally on their Mac computers. This initiative provides a desktop application for accessing AI models, similar to LM Studio.

items.title: New US homeownership measure puts people first
items.summary: A new measure of US homeownership aims to prioritize people's needs, focusing on aspects beyond traditional economic indicators. This approach seeks to provide a more human-centric view of housing.

items.title: Qwen-Image-3.0: Rich Content, Authentic Details, Deep Knowledge
items.summary: Qwen-Image-3.0 is a new AI image generation model that promises rich content, authentic details, and deep knowledge in its outputs. This release highlights advancements in generative AI for images.

items.title: Show HN: Immersive Gaussian Splat tour of grace cathedral, San Francisco
items.summary: An immersive Gaussian Splat tour of Grace Cathedral in San Francisco has been created, showcasing advanced 3D rendering and visualization techniques. This project offers a new way to experience virtual spaces.

items.title: A homeless man was charged with a felony for camping. He's one of the first
items.summary: A homeless man in Tennessee was charged with a felony for camping in a public park after refusing to leave, making him one of the first under rising state-level camping bans. Such bans are increasing nationwide as states try to curb homelessness.

items.title: Amid nurse shortage, a university rolls out the welcome mat for men
items.summary: To address a nurse shortage, a university is actively recruiting men into nursing programs, a field where only about 1 in 8 nurses are male. Increased efforts and a strong job market are contributing to a growing share of male nurses.

items.title: Amid U.S.-Iran war, Houthi rebels threaten to blockade another key strait
items.summary: Amidst the U.S.-Iran conflict, Yemen's Houthi militia, aligned with Iran, has announced a naval blockade of the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, a crucial waterway. This move further escalates regional maritime tensions.

items.title: Arizona primaries set the stage for contested fall races in a swing state
items.summary: Arizona's primaries are concluding, setting up contested fall elections in the swing state, with Democrats choosing candidates for a Republican-held House seat and Republicans selecting their nominee for governor. These races will be crucial in the upcoming midterms.

items.title: How soccer brings a Bhutanese community together in Harrisburg, Pa.
items.summary: Soccer serves as a unifying force for the Nepali-speaking Bhutanese refugee community in Harrisburg, Pennsylvania, helping generations find connection, belonging, and a way to preserve their shared history. It provides a vital social and cultural link.

items.title: India's youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown
items.summary: India's youth-led "Cockroach movement," which began as satirical protest, is committed to continuing its challenge against Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government despite a police crackdown. The movement has gained national attention.

items.title: Iran-backed Houthis announce blockade. And, Cyclospora source remains unknown
items.summary: Iran-backed Houthis have announced a blockade of a Red Sea waterway, escalating regional tensions. Separately, the FDA is still investigating Taylor Farms lettuce as a potential source for a Cyclospora outbreak despite a retracted test result.

items.title: The best economic ideas from around the world — Planet Money Summer School is back
items.summary: NPR's "Planet Money Summer School" is returning with a free economics course, offering listeners insights into great economic ideas from around the world. The series aims to educate on global economic concepts.

items.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian goods
items.summary: President Donald Trump has imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, asserting that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol, and dairy products. This action increases trade friction between the two countries.

items.title: Nancy Mace won’t run for Lindsey Graham’s Senate seat
items.summary: Republican Rep. Nancy Mace announced she will not run for the South Carolina Senate seat previously held by the late Lindsey Graham, prioritizing her current fight and effectively guaranteeing she won't hold elected office after her term ends. Lindsey Graham's sister, Darline Graham, who was appointed to the seat, is running for a full term with Trump's endorsement.

items.title: Nativ: Run AI models locally on your Mac
items.summary: Nativ is a new macOS desktop application developed by Prince Canuma, designed to run vision-LLMs and other AI models locally on a Mac. It provides a chat interface and a localhost API server, building on the MLX library.

items.title: Reverse-engineering is cheap now
items.summary: The cost and effort of reverse-engineering and automating home devices have significantly decreased due to coding agents. This makes experimenting with undocumented APIs more viable, as the psychological baggage of maintenance or redoing work is reduced.

items.title: Who’s Afraid of Chinese Models?
items.summary: Ben Thompson proposes US laws to clarify fair use for AI data training and ban terms prohibiting model distillation, to help US open models compete with Chinese counterparts. This comes as China emphasizes open-source AI, exemplified by Alibaba's Qwen 3.8 Max release.

items.title: Democratic fields are shifting; G.O.P. bank accounts are growing
items.summary: Democratic political fields are shifting, with particular movements in Maine following Graham Platner's departure and complications in Wisconsin's Democratic primary for governor. Meanwhile, Republican candidates are reportedly seeing growth in their fundraising accounts.

items.title: After TikTok, Snap settles social media addiction case
items.summary: Snap has reached a tentative settlement in a lawsuit alleging social media platforms are harmful to children and young adults, following a similar settlement by TikTok. This agreement avoids a trial scheduled for later this month.

items.title: AI music generator Suno breach affects 55M users, per Have I Been Pwned
items.summary: The AI music generator Suno experienced a data breach, compromising the names, phone numbers, and physical addresses of 55 million users. This incident was reported by "Have I Been Pwned."

items.title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
items.summary: A key AI protocol is adopting a "stateless" approach to session IDs on the server side, similar to how most websites operate, making it easier to use. This change simplifies interaction with the protocol.

items.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
items.summary: Anthropic's $1.5 billion copyright settlement has received final approval, resolving one specific legal case but not the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models. The settlement addresses claims related to AI training data.

items.title: Bluecore Energy raises $10M to build portable nuclear reactors on barges
items.summary: Bluecore Energy, a maritime nuclear energy startup, secured $10 million in pre-seed funding to develop portable nuclear reactors that will be built on barges. This initiative aims to provide flexible and movable power generation.

items.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
items.summary: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly developing a new AI chip specifically designed to improve the efficiency of its Gemini models. This internal chip development aims to optimize AI performance.

items.title: Gritt exits stealth with $32 million for robots to build solar plants — then, everything else
items.summary: Gritt has emerged from stealth with $34 million in funding, intending to use robots to automate difficult tasks on construction sites, starting with solar plant construction. The company plans to expand this automation to other construction areas eventually.

items.title: Judge pauses $110B Paramount-Warner Bros. merger
items.summary: A judge has paused the $110 billion merger between Paramount and Warner Bros., following a lawsuit alleging the deal would harm competition for movie theaters, basic cable distributors, and audiences. This action aims to prevent potential monopolistic effects.

items.title: Light made a flip phone. It’s colorful and it’s cheap.
items.summary: Light has launched a new, affordable and colorful flip phone, aiming to modernize the form factor. The company's co-founder, Kaiwei Tang, expressed surprise at the renewed relevance of flip phones.

items.title: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
items.summary: Deezer, a music streaming service, reports that over 50% of its daily uploads in June were AI-generated tracks, totaling more than 90,000 artificial intelligence-created songs uploaded daily. This indicates a significant increase in AI's role in music production.

items.title: Natural raises $30M to reinvent payments for AI agents — and take on Stripe
items.summary: Natural, a one-year-old startup, has raised $30 million to develop new financial architecture for autonomous AI transactions, aiming to compete with established payment processing companies like Stripe. The company seeks to revolutionize payments for AI agents.

items.title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
items.summary: The discussion around banning Chinese-made open-weight Large Language Models (LLMs) highlights the challenges of commercializing AI, especially as OpenAI expresses concerns about such models. This debate brings into focus the competition and geopolitical implications in the AI sector.

items.title: The Xteink X4 Pro could be the tiny e-reader of your dreams
items.summary: The Xteink X4 Pro e-reader is enhanced with a touchscreen and front light, making the pocket-sized device more appealing. These features improve its usability and reading experience.

items.title: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
items.summary: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door, with Trump's latest AI czar resigning. This follows David Sacks's departure from the position.

items.title: UK government scraps plans for digital ID cards after millions of Brits opposed
items.summary: The UK government has abandoned plans for a digital ID card program after facing significant public opposition. This decision was made in favor of focusing on a tax cut to address the cost of living.

items.title: What to know about the landmark Warner Bros. Discovery sale
items.summary: Paramount's proposed $111 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery is a major Hollywood deal currently under scrutiny. This landmark merger continues to develop amidst legal challenges.

items.title: X relaunches a rebuilt Android app after year-long effort
items.summary: X (formerly Twitter) has relaunched a rebuilt version of its Android app globally, following a year-long development effort. This update aims to improve the app's performance and user experience.

items.title: How Trump Decided to Go to War with Iran, with Jonathan Swan
items.summary: Jonathan Swan discusses his book "Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump," exploring how Donald Trump makes geopolitical decisions, particularly his rationale for joining Israel's war against Iran. The book delves into the former president's foreign policy thinking.

items.title: I Was in the DSA. Here’s What They Really Believe.
items.summary: A former member of the Democratic Socialists of America (DSA) describes a rise in extremism within the organization, including collaboration with Leninist groups and animosity towards the U.S. and West. The DSA's new platform includes aims such as ending the presidency, abolishing prisons, universal free housing, and lifting sanctions on Cuba, Venezuela, and Iran.

items.title: Meet the ‘Anti-Platners’: Real Blue-Collar Democrats
items.summary: Independent Senate candidate Dan Osborn is distancing himself from former consultants tied to the imploded campaign of Graham Platner, who faced rape allegations. Osborn, a blue-collar Democrat, emphasizes his distinct values and aims to differentiate himself from the "Platner" faction of the Democratic party.

items.title: This Week in Canada: Why a Social Media Ban Isn’t the Answer
items.summary: Max Anderson, a 13-year-old, highlights how Canadian teenagers use social media for learning and interests beyond socializing, expressing concern over Bill C-34 which proposes a ban for those under 16. Despite widespread support for age restrictions among Canadian teenagers, there are arguments against a blanket ban.

items.title: Epictetus on Love and Loss: The Stoic Strategy for Surviving Heartbreak
items.summary: Stoic philosopher Epictetus taught that to cope with love and loss, one should accept the impermanence of all things and cherish them while present, rather than clinging to expectations of eternity. This approach aims to transform grief into appreciation for what was.

items.title: How to Love the World More: George Saunders on the Courage of Uncertainty
items.summary: George Saunders argues that human certainty, driven by our inherent need to create self-serving narratives, often leads to misunderstandings and hinders a full appreciation of the world. He suggests that embracing uncertainty, like an artist's doubt, fosters deeper understanding and empathy.

items.title: A touchscreen and light make the new X4 Pro the best version of Xteink’s tiny e-readers
items.summary: The new Xteink X4 Pro e-reader features a touchscreen and built-in lighting, making it the most portable and user-friendly version of the company's devices, despite some hardware and software compromises. While priced higher and requiring an adapter for USB-C, it offers a vastly improved experience, especially with third-party firmware support.

items.title: Halliday’s latest smart glasses feature a much-improved display
items.summary: Halliday's Gen 2 smart glasses feature a significantly improved display compared to their original model, replacing the "finicky" movable display window with a more traditional design. This update addresses past issues with visibility and user comfort.

items.title: Samsung can’t afford to play it safe with Apple’s first foldable looming
items.summary: Samsung is expected to launch new foldable phones, including a new, wider form factor, at its upcoming Galaxy Unpacked event. This move is seen as necessary to innovate as Apple's first foldable iPhone approaches, challenging Samsung's long-standing leadership in the foldable market.

items.title: Stellantis taps Mobileye for hands-free driving assist
items.summary: Stellantis announced it will integrate Intel's Mobileye technology, including its EyeQ system-on-a-chip and Road Experience Management program, for Level 2 hands-free driving features starting in 2027. This system will enable hands-free, eyes-on driving and intelligent lane keeping on unmarked roads.

items.title: The Light Flip is a minimalist flip phone with a point to prove
items.summary: Light has introduced the Light Flip, a minimalist flip phone that aligns with the company's anti-smartphone ethos, contrasting with their previous, more smartphone-like device. It targets users, especially younger ones, who desire a device that doesn't resemble a smartphone.

items.title: The Switch 2 is $50 off at Woot for new customers
items.summary: The Nintendo Switch 2 is available for $50 off for new Woot customers, bringing the price down from $449.99, ahead of a planned price increase to $499.99 on September 1st, 2026. This discount offers a rare opportunity for savings on the console.
